content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN sets of integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. For each valid iii, let's denote the elements of the set AiA_iAi​ by {ci,1,ci,2,…,ci,∣Ai∣}\{c_{i,1}, c_{i,2}, \ldots, c_{i,|A_i|}\}{ci,1​,ci,2​,…,ci,∣Ai​∣​}.

Find the number of ways to choose a sequence (a1,a2,…,aN)(a_1, a_2, \ldots, a_N)(a1​,a2​,…,aN​) such that:


ai∈Aia_i \in A_iai​∈Ai​ for each valid iii
ai≠ai+1a_i \neq a_{i+1}ai​=ai+1​ for each valid iii and a1≠aNa_1 \neq a_Na1​=aN​
Since this number may be large, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: 1≤ci,j≤2000001 \le c_{i,j} \le 2000001≤ci,j​≤200000 for each valid i,ji, ji,j
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains an integer ∣Ai∣|A_i|∣Ai​∣ followed by a space and ∣Ai∣|A_i|∣Ai​∣ space-separated non-negative integers ci,1,ci,2,…,ci,∣Ai∣c_{i,1}, c_{i,2}, \ldots, c_{i,|A_i|}ci,1​,ci,2​,…,ci,∣Ai​∣​.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ― the number of ways to choose (a1,a2,…,aN)(a_1, a_2, \ldots, a_N)(a1​,a2​,…,aN​), modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
